reforms.description: The Spanish authorities launched the InfoXXI plan, which includes significant funding for ICT initiatives aimed at promoting the new economy, including high-speed Internet access for schools and a special network for researchers. Additionally, the 2001 Budget includes tax breaks for small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) investing in new technologies and R&D, aimed at stimulating innovation and economic growth. A new regulation on venture capital was also approved, introducing a favorable tax regime that exempts 99% of capital gains from corporate income tax for at least two years.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The combination of funding for ICT initiatives, tax breaks for SMEs, and favorable venture capital regulations is expected to enhance innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant but narrower changes aimed at enhancing innovation and supporting SMEs.
reforms.source_quote: The plan includes many measures, such as a high speed Internet link for all schools, a special Internet network for researchers, and the generalisation of the use of the Internet between the public administration and the general public.

reforms.description: Spain plans to increase investment in innovation and R&D by 14.1% as part of its budget priorities, reflecting a commitment to enhance its research capabilities. This includes the establishment of a new Ministry for Science and Technology tasked with boosting scientific and technological development.
reforms.component_name: Increased R&D Funding and Institutional Support
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The increase in R&D funding and the establishment of a dedicated ministry are expected to significantly enhance Spain's research capabilities and innovation output.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a new ministry and a substantial increase in R&D funding represent a structural change in Spain's approach to innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Measures taken by the government boost scientific and technology development, including the creation of a new Ministry (Ministry for Science and Technology) with specific tasks in this matter.

reforms.description: The Spanish government has implemented a new venture capital law aimed at promoting the emergence of small and dynamic firms, enhancing the startup ecosystem.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The new venture capital law is expected to stimulate investment in startups, fostering innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law has been enacted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The law represents a significant but narrower change aimed at enhancing the startup ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The emergence of small and dynamic firms has been favoured by a new venture capital law in 1998.

reforms.description: In June 2000, the holding period for venture capital companies to enjoy tax exemptions was reduced from two years to one, further incentivizing investment in innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reduction in the holding period for tax exemptions is expected to encourage more investment in innovative firms, enhancing the overall innovation landscape.
reforms.status_evidence: The adjustment has been enacted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: This adjustment is a minor change aimed at improving the existing venture capital framework.
reforms.source_quote: In June 2000 the holding period to enjoy such an exemption was reduced from two years to one.
